title: Akal Takht asks Punjab govt. to revise anti-sacrilege law, remove clauses contrary to Sikh sentiments
content: The highest Sikh temporal authority says the amendment law was passed without consultations and gives the government a month’s time to re-amend the legislation. Akal Takht’s head says many MLAs admitted to have not read the draft of the legislation while passing it

title: CM Patel inaugurates VGRC in Vadodara, calls for investment-led growth
content: The conference was attended by Union Minister Manohar Lal Khattar, Deputy Chief Minister Harsh Sanghavi, State Ministers, senior bureaucrats, ambassadors and representatives from industry

title: SIR: No documents needed during house-to-house enumeration process, says Karnataka Chief Electoral Officer
content: Over 5.54 crore electors to be covered in month-long house-to-house drive beginning June 30; draft rolls on August 5

title: Catching up on border infrastructure, says Kiren Rijiju after tribal group claims land grab by China
content: We have seen some media reports alleging recent encroachment by Chinese PLA and setting up of camps in Arunachal Pradesh; these reports are incorrect and without any basis, read a statement by the Indian Army

title: Press Club of India raises concern over passport issues faced by two senior journalists
content: The Press Club says deletion of former Editor of The Telegraph R. Rajagopal’s name from the electoral roll in West Bengal reportedly led to non-renewal of his passport for over 100 days allegedly following an ‘adverse’ police report

title: The sight of six burnt workers will never leave me, says Sigachi blast survivor
content: A year after the Sigachi Industries explosion at Pashamylaram claimed 54 lives, survivor Divya recounts the haunting memories that refuse to fade

title: JKLF chief Yasin Malik among five named in charge-sheet in 1990 Sarla Bhat killing case
content: The State Investigation Agency revealed the involvement of Malik, then JKLF chief commander; Khurshid Ahmad Chalkoo; Abdul Hamid Sheikh; Mohammad Yousuf Sofi alias Idrees and Ghulam Mohammad Taploo ‘in planning and executing the abduction and brutal killing’

title: One year on, Sigachi blast victims’ families seek justice and promised ex gratia
content: Widows, relatives of the victims and activists gathered at Pashamylaram to demand full payment of the promised ₹1 crore compensation, alleging they have received only about ₹41.5 lakh each

title: Misrepresentation of facts by prosecutor led  to bail for accused in officials’ assault case: ED
content: State government had removed Geena Kumari, who was appointed during the term of the previous LDF government, from the post following the controversy

title: West Bengal govt. to introduce Uniform Civil Code Bill soon
content: Committee led by retired judge of Supreme Court Justice Ranjana Prakash Desai to study State’s family laws and submit report in four weeks; tribal communities to be excluded from the law

title: Sports quota candidates unable to make optional entry in mock CET rounds with final list yet to be submitted
content: KEA authorities say they have written multiple letters to the DYES, urging it to submit the final list of eligible candidates

title: Several people injured in group clash at Naregal village of Haveri district
content: A quarrel that started over bursting of crackers during a bull procession organised as part of Kara Hunnime escalated and led to the group clash

title: President Murmu to grace CTUAP’s first convocation; 373 students to receive degrees
content: The Visakhapatnam university will award 373 degrees and 13 gold medals, with Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and Governor S. Abdul Nazeer expected to attend

title: Revised textbooks with correct content to be distributed in schools: Odisha Minister
content: The CM constituted an inquiry committee, which conducted an investigation, and action has been taken against those found responsible for the negligence, says Odisha School and Mass Education Minister Nityananda Gond

title: President Murmu to attend first convocation of Central University of A.P. on July 1
content: A total of 1,268 students from the graduating batches of 2020-2026 will receive degrees, including 480 undergraduate and 788 postgraduate students representing 23 Indian states

title: PM Family Care Tracker launched in Gujarat to digitally monitor maternal and child health
content: Introduced as a pilot project in Gujarat, beginning with Gandhinagar, the platform seeks to integrate health, nutrition, and welfare services by tracking pregnant women, newborns, infants, children, and adolescents up to 18 years of age.

title: Over 1,300 dead in Europe: How 'Omega Block' supercharged a deadly heatwave
content: A severe heatwave gripping Europe has claimed over 1,300 lives since June 21, with France reporting nearly 1,000 excess deaths and 74 drownings. Nations like Germany, the Czech Republic, and Poland have shattered temperature records. This extreme heat, amplified by an Omega Block weather pattern, is straining infrastructure and highlighting the vulnerability of European buildings not designed for such sustained warmth.

title: Ketan murder case: Cab driver says Siya was 'reluctant' to leave for Bali, argued with brother
content: Investigators are scrutinizing the days leading up to realtor Ketan Agarwal's alleged murder, focusing on his relationship with fiancée Siya Goyal. A planned Bali trip was disrupted when Ketan's passport vanished, with police suspecting Siya's involvement due to opposition from Chetan Chaudhary, with whom she allegedly had a relationship. Both Siya and Chaudhary are arrested, accused of plotting Agarwal's murder.

title: 'Not only Iyer's fault': Ex-cricketer urges India to 'address elephant in the room'
content: Former India batter Manoj Tiwary has questioned who will take responsibility for India's 2-0 T20I series defeat to Ireland, arguing that captain Shreyas Iyer should not be made the sole scapegoat. Tiwary urged critics to "address the elephant in the room," indirectly pointing towards the team management and coaching staff led by Gautam Gambhir for the disappointing result.

title: Bought 4 years ago, still waiting: The supply chain shock behind Hyderabad’s 63,000 delayed homes
content: In Hyderabad, a staggering number of homebuyers find themselves in a predicament, as delays have affected nearly 63,000 housing units, some postponed by over a year. This alarming situation arises from supply chain issues linked to ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, alongside increasing costs and a shortage of labor

title: Elon Musk says feminism and lack of children has killed the happiness of ...
content: Elon Musk has ignited another debate, asserting that feminism and the choice to forgo children have diminished happiness. He believes declining birth rates threaten civilization, citing historical examples and India's falling fertility rate as evidence. Musk, a father of 14, consistently champions family's importance and has previously predicted AI might paradoxically boost birth rates.

title: 'Russia might launch limited war against Nato within ... ': Dutch intel sounds alarm
content: A stark warning from Dutch defense intelligence suggests Russia might launch a limited military strike against a NATO member within a year of the Ukraine war's conclusion. This assessment, highlighting Russia's preparations for a prolonged European confrontation, echoes concerns from NATO's Secretary General.  As Europe reassesses its security, the report urges immediate investment in military readiness and advanced technologies to navigate this uncertain 'grey area' between peace and conflict.

title: 'We're too used to hitting sixes': India coach doesn't sugarcoat Ireland humiliation
content: "I think there is a little bit of disbelief, we have just won a World Cup. We have been outdone, outsmarted by a team which did their basics very well. Firstly credit to Ireland and secondly the learning for us (is) in terms of being able to adapt to different conditions and against different teams and that is the biggest takeaway from this series," Ten Doeschate said at the post-match press conference.

title: Jeff Bezos vs Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

title: 'Deficiency in service': Consumer panel orders NHAI to compensate motorist over pothole damage
content: A Nagpur consumer commission has ruled that collecting toll without maintaining roads is a 'deficiency in service'. The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) was ordered to compensate a motorist whose car was damaged by a pothole. The commission emphasized that providing safe roads is a toll collector's responsibility, and vehicle damage due to poor road conditions warrants compensation for repair costs, distress, and legal expenses.

title: JKLF chief Yasin Malik named main accused in brutal 1990 killing of Kashmiri Pandit nurse Sarla Bhat
content: Nearly 36 years after the brutal killing of Kashmiri Pandit nurse Sarla Bhat, the Jammu and Kashmir State Investigation Agency has filed a chargesheet naming JKLF chief Yasin Malik as the principal accused. Bhat was abducted and murdered in 1990 on suspicion of being a police informer. The SIA described the development as a historic milestone.

title: Katie Miller is not impressed with MacKenzie Scott who has donated billions
content: MacKenzie Scott's substantial charitable donations, totaling over $26 billion, have drawn criticism from figures like Katie Miller and Elon Musk. Miller specifically pointed to Scott's funding of progressive causes, including Planned Parenthood and racial justice groups.  Scott, who received a significant Amazon stake in her divorce, has pledged to donate the majority of her wealth, supporting diverse non-profits across education, healthcare, and climate initiatives.

title: Ben Stokes: The man who refused to leave quietly, then left at tea
content: Stokes was never a man of averages. He belonged to moments, and moments have an inconvenient habit of resisting arithmetic. The 258 at Cape Town, the fastest Test 250 ever scored, tells you more about him than any spreadsheet could. The mean is for actuaries. Stokes dealt in extremes, in tamasha, in the improbable stories grandparents tell children who politely pretend they have never heard them before.

title: Uruguay's FIFA WC exit turns chaotic as federation cancels team flight home
content: Uruguay's 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign ended disastrously as the two-time champions finished bottom of Group H without a win, losing to Spain after draws against Cabo Verde and Saudi Arabia. Reports revealed internal tensions, with players questioning coach Marcelo Bielsa's methods. The federation allegedly cancelled the team's charter flight home, while Bielsa admitted his three-year tenure had "left nothing" due to poor results.

title: Chambal’s dreaded dacoit Jagan Gurjar found dead in high-security Ajmer jail
content: Notorious dacoit Jagan Gurjar was allegedly murdered by a fellow inmate in Ajmer jail. Police reported Gurjar was strangled with a towel by Vishnu, his cellmate, following a dispute. Gurjar, who faced numerous criminal cases and had a history of high-profile incidents, was transferred to the high-security facility in March. Vishnu has reportedly confessed to the crime.

title: Erling Haaland, Isabel Haugseng Johansen's combined net worth in 2026
content: Erling Haaland and Isabel Haugseng Johansen have built one of football's most talked-about partnerships. While Haaland's estimated $100 million net worth comes from his Manchester City contract, bonuses and endorsements, Johansen maintains a private lifestyle with an estimated net worth of $250,000-$300,000. The couple welcomed their first son in 2024 and continue to enjoy a family-focused life. Their combined wealth, luxury properties and growing global profile keep them among football's most prominent young couples.

title: Patrick Mahomes roasted online for flaunting 'dad bod' in new Instagram post
content: Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes is facing online criticism for his physique during the offseason, with some labeling it a 'dad bod.' Despite the body-shaming, Mahomes remains focused on his recovery from a knee injury and his determination to be ready for the 2026 NFL season. He emphasizes his commitment to rehabilitation and daily improvement, aiming to avoid missing games.

title: Emma Raducanu, Carlos Alcaraz romance rumors resurface at Wimbledon
content: Emma Raducanu and Carlos Alcaraz were one of tennis’ most talked-about duos last year after they teamed up for the revamped US Open mixed doubles event. Their close friendship quickly sparked dating rumors, but both players consistently maintained that their relationship was nothing more than a genuine friendship built over years on tour.

title: Housing sales dip 6% in Q1 2026 amid economic uncertainties: Anarock
content: Housing sales across India's top seven cities saw a 6% dip in early 2026, with economic uncertainties and Middle East conflict impacting buyer sentiment. Despite this, developers launched more new homes, signaling long-term optimism. While Delhi-NCR, MMR, and Pune experienced declines, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Kolkata showed modest growth, with Delhi-NCR notably seeing the highest price appreciation.

title: 'No official involvement': India distances itself from reports of Track 2 dialogue with Pak
content: India has clarified its stance on recent reports of a Track 2 dialogue with Pakistan. Foreign secretary Vikram Misri stated that these meetings are private initiatives, devoid of any official Indian government participation or backing. He emphasized that such unofficial interactions are common globally and do not hold significant value or recognition from New Delhi's perspective, urging the public not to view them as official engagements.

title: GCL 2026: Carlsen, Giri, Divya , Humpy to play in same team - full squads here
content: Indian Grandmaster Nihal Sarin emerged as one of the biggest attractions in the Global Chess League Season 4 player draft, joining American Gambits after a bidding battle. The Bengaluru-based tournament, scheduled from September 3-13, will feature top stars including Magnus Carlsen and Viswanathan Anand, who switched teams to join PBG Alaskan Knights, as all six franchises finalised their squads.

title: IBGs, Shaktibaan and Divyastra units: How Army is reshaping itself for future wars
content: The Indian Army is undergoing a significant transformation, shifting towards leaner, technology-driven combat units. New Integrated Battle Groups (IBGs) are being formed, combining various arms for rapid deployment and decisive strikes. Specialized drone units like Shaktibaan Regiments and Divyastra Batteries are also being introduced to enhance surveillance and precision targeting. This modernization aims to create a smarter, more agile force prepared for future multi-domain conflicts.

title: Gold price crash: Why Indian households are rushing to sell their old gold jewellery
content: The trend comes at a time when India remains heavily dependent on imported gold. During FY26, the country imported gold worth nearly $72.4 billion, while recycled gold contributed an estimated 125-150 tonnes in 2025.

title: 'SBI wanted donation staff removed 3 months back': Congress on Ram Temple fund row
content: Calling the arrest of eight people in the case "only a glimpse", Congress leader Pawan Khera targeted those "protecting" the people behind the alleged fund embezzlement. Khera tweaked the RSS-BJP's popular Ram Mandir movement slogan in a piercing attack, saying, "Ayodhya ki loot jhanki hai, Kashi-Mathura abhi baaki hai (the alleged loot in Ayodhya was only a glimpse, Kashi and Mathura were yet to come)".

title: 'Russian troops survive just 20–35 minutes on Ukraine frontlines'
content: Russian frontline soldiers reportedly face a grim reality, with survival times as short as 20-35 minutes amidst intense drone warfare and infantry assaults. Pro-war bloggers highlight the rapid deployment of troops and the devastating impact of Ukrainian long-range strikes on Russian infrastructure, including a recent refinery fire. This escalating conflict is reshaping combat and increasing pressure on Moscow.

title: Sun sets on Bazball: Stokes signs off with defeat as NZ seal series 2-1
content: Chasing 373, England resumed the final day on 103 for 4 but never managed to build the partnerships needed to threaten the target. They were bowled out for 212 shortly after lunch, handing New Zealand their first comeback series win in a three-Test series after losing the opening match.

title: West Bengal’s 'anti-goonda bill' cleared: Who can be detained — why it matters
content: The West Bengal assembly on Monday passed the West Bengal Public Safety and Control of Anti-Social Activities Bill, 2026, with 176 members voting in its favour and 41 against it.

title: Ex-SC judge Ranjana Prakash Desai to lead UCC panel in West Bengal: CM Suvendu
content: CM Adhikari announced that a draft UCC Bill will be placed before the state cabinet on July 2. The bill will be tabled in the assembly later, he said. The proposed legislation seeks to establish a common civil framework governing marriage, divorce, inheritance and adoption irrespective of religion. Ahead of the introduction of the Bill, state BJP chief Samik Bhattacharya asserted that constitutionally protected tribal communities would remain outside its ambit.

title: Comcast to split into two companies in major media shake-up
content: Comcast is splitting into two public companies, separating its media arm, NBCUniversal and Sky, from its core connectivity business. This strategic move aims to sharpen focus amidst industry shifts towards streaming and consolidation. Mike Cavanagh will lead the new media entity, while Michael Angelakis takes the helm of the remaining Comcast. The separation, expected within a year, has already boosted investor confidence.

title: Ireland head coach resigns hours after historic 2-0 T2OI series win over India
content: Ireland completed a 2-0 sweep after beating India by one run in the second T20I on Sunday. It was Ireland's first-ever series win over India in any format. They had won the opening match by 34 runs a few days earlier against the reigning T20 world champions.

title: Madhya Pradesh alcohol data decoded: Women’s consumption rises even as national average falls
content: Madhya Pradesh sees a mixed trend in alcohol consumption, with a slight rise in women drinkers, surpassing the national average, while men's consumption remains just below. Chilled beer has become the state's top choice, overtaking traditional liquors. Concerns over illegal trade and social impacts are growing, fueling political debate as sales and government revenue surge.

title: 'They harass me': Faridabad bizman dies by suicide, blames wife & in-laws in final video
content: A 28-year-old garment trader in Faridabad allegedly died by suicide, posting a video accusing his wife and her family of abuse. Rahul claimed continuous physical and mental harassment, including assault and a false case. His family discovered him after seeing the video. Police have registered a case against the wife and her relatives and are investigating the allegations.

title: 'Ram ko loota, desh ko loota': Sibal attacks BJP over Ayodhya temple 'donation' case
content: Rajya Sabha MP Kapil Sibal criticised the BJP government, alleging corruption and betrayal of faith regarding Ram Temple donations. He urged voters to oust the ruling party, citing broken promises and the "Achhe Din" slogan as false. This comes as an SIT investigates alleged embezzlement, leading to eight arrests. The Supreme Court will hear a related petition in July.

title: 'McCullum has got to go with Stokes': Ex-England pacer lambasts current Test setup
content: Former England pacer and Ashes winner Steve Harmison has called for England head coach Brendon McCullum to step down following Ben Stokes' retirement. Harmison criticised McCullum's "Bazball" approach, calling it "amateur" and arguing that Stokes and McCullum were a joint project. Meanwhile, Stokes said he retired due to burnout and to rediscover his love for cricket.

title: Who is Puneet Sharma, the Axis Bank CFO who has resigned?
content: Axis Bank's Chief Financial Officer, Puneet Sharma, has resigned, stepping down on Sunday, June 28, but will continue until August 31. Sharma, who joined in March 2020, oversaw finance, legal, and investor relations, during which the bank's stock nearly doubled. He is leaving to pursue new professional opportunities. Sharma brings over two decades of finance experience from previous roles at Tata Capital and Citibank.

title: Ajay Devgn’s ‘Chauhaan’ faces backlash over alleged misuse of Chauhan clan name
content: Ajay Devgn's 'Chauhaan' faces fresh controversy as the Kshatriya Parishad accuses filmmakers of misusing Rajput history and identity for political gain. The organization decries the 'weaponisation of Rajput history,' asserting that the Chauhan legacy belongs to history, not partisan campaigns. This follows earlier criticism of the teaser's portrayal of the Kashmir conflict and a dialogue about pellet guns.

title: Safran vs Rolls-Royce: The AMCA engine race that could shape India's aerospace autonomy
content: ​India’s strive to be “atmanirbhar” in engines is not a story of 2026. In 1986, the government had tasked DRDO's Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE) with building the indigenous Kaveri engine to power the then-nascent Tejas light combat aircraft. What followed was four decades of heartbreak. Despite nine prototype engines, over 3,200 hours of testing, and a budget overrun of 642 per cent, the Kaveri produced only 70.4 kilonewtons (kN) of wet thrust against a required 81 kilonewtons.

title: Moondra spent hours studying Sooryavanshi; Sanju, Abhishek, Shreyas paid the price
content: From spending hours plotting against Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to dismantling India's star-studded batting line-up, Jai Moondra's dream debut for Ireland was years in the making. His childhood coach and mother recount the relentless work ethic that transformed an engineering student from Tonk, Rajasthan into Ireland's hero in their historic series win over India.

title: 'He sacrificed his career': Brother defends Champat Rai amid Ram Temple row
content: Champat Rai's brother Sunil Bansal said his brother began professional career as a teacher in Rohtak during 1970-71 before joining RSM Degree College in Dhampur as a physics lecturer in 1972. His remarks came after Champat Rai resigned as the trust's general secretary amid allegations of large-scale embezzlement of devotees' donations, with questions being raised over his alleged role in the matter.

title: Chief Minister calls for speedy execution of Amaravati works
content: Naidu reviews ₹50,999-crore project, urges contractors to maintain quality and meet timelines while inviting reputed firms to partner in building the greenfield capital

title: Minister urges tobacco buyers to maintain prices above ₹200 per Kg
content: Gottipati Ravi Kumar directs officials to ensure that all registered companies participate in the bidding process and closely monitor auction centres to prevent situations where farmers receive inadequate or no bids

title: JSP launches grassroots membership drive in Anantapur
content: Addressing a committee meeting, party’s observer for Anantapur parliamentary constituency says any enthusiastic person wishing to work for JSP can submit their application

title: Kerala State election chief’s appointment kicks up political row
content: KPCC general secretary P.M. Niyas demands inspection of intelligence reports on N. Seshadrinathan, the government nominee for the post, claiming the latter has ‘Sangh Parivar links’; Minister K.M. Shaji defends appointment

title: Sabarimala gold theft: SIT’s interim report mentions criminal conspiracy by TDB officials
content: High Court permits special investigation team to register fresh case, including against former TDB president P.S. Prashanth

title: Naidu to launch welfare, development projects during five-day tour
content: Pension distribution programme, Hero MotoCorp facility, VB-G RAMG launch and JSW Rayalaseema Steel Plant among key events in Tirupati, Chittoor, Kadapa and SPSR Nellore districts

title: Nandyal celebrates ‘Eruvaka Pournami’ to kick-start agricultural season
content: Participating in farming activities, Nandyal MP urges paddy farmers to opt for crops that require less water and to use intercropping methods to mitigate the impact of El Niño

title: BJP Has No "Sharam" Or "Dharam": Akhilesh Yadav On Ram Temple Theft Row
content: "The government itself has accepted this fact, that such an incident has occurred there," Akhilesh Yadav told NDTV in an exclusive interview today.

title: Not Cyborgs Yet. AI Gives Humans Super Assist In a Swiss Lab Breakthrough
content: In his book "AI to Eye" Professor Riener argues that Artificial Intelligence has the potential to make the world more inclusive, but only if it is designed with people at its center.

title: On Camera, Trader Robbed Of Gold, Cash, Car On Highway In Mangaluru
content: A seven-member gang intercepted the car and fled with the vehicle carrying around 180 grams of gold and cash worth an estimated Rs 20 lakh.

title: In Major Ruling, US Supreme Court 'Supercharges' Donald Trump's Powers
content: The justices allowed Fed governor Lisa Cook to stay in her job while she fights the Republican president's effort to fire her over allegations of mortgage fraud, which she has denied.

title: 'Women's Safety Non-Negotiable': Vijay To Officials In His 1st Officers' Meet
content: Vijay declared there would be "no change and no compromise" on women's safety, directing collectors and SPs to accord it special attention and ensure prompt investigation of offences.

title: Majority Of India-Bound Ships 'Go Dark' In Hormuz Amid Iran Threat
content: Data from maritime intelligence firm Kpler shows that nearly 62% of tankers and cargo vessels sailing from the Persian Gulf to India turned off their transponders while crossing the strait.

title: Live Updates: Major Police Op In Germany's Stade After 6 Dead In Mass Shooting
content: Atleast 5 people were killed after a shooting rocked the northern German city of Stade on Monday, triggering a massive police operation.

title: 6 Dead In Shooting In Germany, 2 Arrested
content: According to police, shots were fired near a youth facility, and they are currently conducting a large-scale operation outside the town centre.

title: Delhi EV Policy 2026 Cleared: No Hybrid Benefits, EV-Only Push Gets Bigger
content: The policy, which comes into effect from July 1 and will remain in force till March 31, 2030, lays out a roadmap for accelerating electric mobility through purchase subsidies, scrapping incentives, tax exemptions, charging infrastructure and phased registration deadlines for key vehicle segments.

title: CBI Arrests Fugitive Wanted In 2021 Post-Poll Violence Case In Bengal
content: Khalid-Uz-Zaman never joined the investigation. He was arrested from Yetala village in Nanded by the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI), a spokesperson of the agency said.

title: Ahmedabad Begins 149th Rath Yatra Celebrations At Jagannath Temple
content: The official countdown to the historic 149th Rath Yatra in Ahmedabad commenced with spiritual fervor on the auspicious day of Jeth Sud Purnima on June 29, 2026.

title: Video: Slapped By Friend, Gujarat Man Climbs Mobile Tower, Demands Apology
content: The incident triggered a rescue operation involving the police and fire brigade, while a large crowd gathered below the tower, urging him to come down.

title: ISKCON Kolkata Removes Vice President Radharaman Das Amid Midday Meal Row
content: The development comes amidst heated debates over eggs being dropped from the midday meal scheme in West Bengal.

title: Andhra Pradesh Allots 120.76 Acres For Rocket Fuel Plant In Anantapur
content: The Andhra Pradesh government has approved the allotment of 120.76 acres of land to SpaceFields Pvt. Ltd. to set up a Solid Propellant (Rocket Fuel) Processing Facility at the Industrial Park in Thimmasamudram, Anantapur district.

title: Delhi's Wait For Monsoon To Continue As Weather Office Predicts Slow Advance
content: Delhi residents will have to wait a little longer for the southwest monsoon, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) saying in its latest forecast on Monday that there is no likelihood of the monsoon reaching the national capital over the next five days.

title: Rekha Gupta On Delhi's EV Push, Anti-Pollution Plan At NDTV Conclave
content: On the issue of pollution, Chief Minister Rekha Gupta said her government has over the last year "prepared a very scientific roadmap" to combat it.

title: Stray Dog With Puppies On Campus Forces Kerala School To Shut For A Day
content: Choondal Grama Panchayat President Siny Prasad said while stray dog-catching squads had managed to capture other dogs in the area, the mother dog had so far evaded them.

title: Amid Heatwave, Delhi Peak Power Demand Reaches All-Time High Of 8,748 MW
content: According to the real-time data of the State Load Dispatch Centre, Delhi's peak power demand clocked 8,748 MW at 15:17 pm, the highest ever for the city.
